english: S.g. — 4. genitals, Wdn. and elsewh.; dbáṅ-po lág-pa (dbang po lag pa) v. dbaṅ-lág (dbang lag) sub dbaṅ (dbang).

Lex. = tog (tog) and rtsé-mo (rtse mo) top, summit, point e.g. of a mčod-rten (mchod rten) Glr.; the point, or acc. to some the grooves of the púr-pa (phur pa) or exorcising dagger; rtai (rta'i) dbal (dbal) bzaṅ-ṅan (bzang ngan) Lex.? — dbál-ba (dbal ba) v. °bal-ba ('bal ba).

resp. for mgo (mgo), head, frq.; beginning, commencement, e.g. of holy doctrine Glr.; *'u lán-cè* W., the mode of greeting between Lamas, by touching each other with their fore-heads; to bless (a layman by imposition of hands); dbu (dbu) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) to be the head, the principal person, e.g. in an assembly of believers Mil.; more definitely: dbu (dbu) mdzád-do (mdzad do) he was my instructor Mil. — dbu-skrá (dbu skra) the hair of the head. — dbu-rgyán (dbu rgyan) ornament of the head, diadem Mil. — dbu-rṅás (dbu rngas) Sch. pillow. — dbu-čàn (dbu can) furnished with a head, i.e. with a thick stroke at the top (of a letter), hence the name of the Tibetan printing characters. — dbu-čén (dbu chen) 1. higher officer. 2. dbu-čuṅ (dbu chung) subaltern officer Cs. — dbu-rǰe (dbu rje) Reverence, Reverend, title of Lamas. — dbu-snyúṅ (dbu snyung) bžés-pa (bzhes pa) Sch., dbu-snyúṅ (dbu snyung) daṅ (dang) bro (bro) °bór-ba ('bor ba) Glr. resp. to swear by one's head. — dbu-tód (dbu thod) royal cap, crown. — dbu-mtún (dbu mthun) drúṅ-du (drung du) resp. the same as žabs (zhabs) drúṅ-du (drung du) in directing letters: To... — dbu-°páṅ (dbu 'phang) elevation, high rank, dignity, stód-pa (stod pa) to praise, smád-pa (smad pa) to despise, to revile (dignities). — dbú-ma (dbu ma) 1. n. of the goddess Durga, the wife of Siva. 2. principal vein, v. rtsá-ba (rtsa ba). 3. the middle (-doctrine), middle-road, which endeavours to avoid the two extremes Was., also dbú-mai (dbu ma'i) lam (lam) or ltá-ba (lta ba); dbú-ma-pa (dbu ma pa) an adherent of this doctrine Sch., cf. however mdo (mdo) extr. — dbu-méd (dbu med) the Tibetan current hand-writing, cf. dbú-čan (dbu can). — dbu-rmóg (dbu rmog) Zam. w.e.; in W. *gyúl-po u'-móg čo žig* is said to signify: Long live the king! — dbu-rtsé (dbu rtse) the top, pinnacle, of a temple, monastery Glr. — dbu-mdzád (dbu mdzad) (cf. dbu (dbu) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) above) chairman, principal, warden, in convents an official that takes the lead in performing the prayers. — dbu-zwá (dbu zwa) cap — dbu-šóg (dbu shog) title-page Sch.

1. breath, respiration, dbugs (dbugs) rṅúb-pa (rngub pa) daṅ (dang) °byín-pa ('byin pa) or °byúṅ-ba ('byung ba) to respire, to inhale and exhale air Med., W. *tón-cè* for °byúṅ-ba ('byung ba); dbugs (dbugs) °byín-pa ('byin pa) to stop for rest, to recover one's breath Sch. (and perh. Pth.); dbugs-dbyúṅ (dbugs dbyung) ṭób-pa (thob pa) to be eased in one's mind, after despondency Tar.; *'ug gyaṅ bór-cè* W. to stop, to keep back one's breath; *'ug sub* or *kor táṅ-cè* W. to choke, suffocate, strangle, throttle; skyé-°gro (skye 'gro) tams-čád-kyi (thams cad kyi) dbugs (dbugs) lén-pa (len pa) to take away the breath of beings (which is ascribed to the demon pe-dkár (pe dkar)) Glr.; ṗyí-dbugs (phyi dbugs) seems to be the last breath of a dying man, but náṅ-dbugs (nang dbugs) is some fantastic physiological notion Thgr.; dbugs (dbugs) mdé-ba (mde ba) and mi-bdé-ba (mi bde ba) an easy and a hard breathing Med. frq.; dbugs-túṅ (dbugs thung) short breath; dbugs (dbugs) rdzáṅ-ba (rdzang ba) or brdzáṅs-pa (brdzangs pa) shortness of breath, asthma, as a complaint of old age Thgy.; dbugs (dbugs) lheb-lhéb (lheb lheb) byéd-pa (byed pa) to pant, to be pursy Med. — dbugs-rgód (dbugs rgod) Lt.? — dbugs-ṅán (dbugs ngan) Sch. flatulence. — dbugs (dbugs) ṭebs-rél (thebs rel) Sch. 'in one breath'? — 2. a breath, one respiration, as smallest measure of time = 1/360 ǩyím (khyim) = 4 seconds.
termin. of dbu (dbu), Sch. first, at first.

also °úr-ba ('ur ba), 'úr-ba ('ur ba) to smooth, šóg-bu (shog bu) paper, ras (ras) woollen stuff, yžál (yzhal) a pavement Cs.; *'ur gyág-pa* C., *gyáb-cè* W. to iron, to smooth linen etc., *'ur-čag* smoothing-iron.

I. vb. v. °búl-ba ('bul ba). — II. adj. poor, indigent Dzl.; sbst. poverty, want, penury, dbúl-ba (dbul ba) sel-ba (sel ba) to relieve want Glr.; dbul-žiṅ (dbul zhing) °póṅs-pai ('phongs pa'i) rigs (rigs) a poor and indigent generation Dzl.; hence frq. dbúl-póṅs (dbul phongs) poor, a poor man, pauper Mil.; poverty Glr.; usually dbúl-po (dbul po), fem. dbúl-mo (dbul mo), poor.
(Ld. *'us*, C. *'ụ**) 1. middle, midst, centre, tág-pai (thag pa'i) dbus (dbus) tsám-du (tsam du)
